topic_label: Multimodal NLP × Genomic Foundation Models (DNA / RNA)
thesis: Combining natural-language models with DNA / RNA foundation models opens a new design space: text grounds biological sequences with literature context, and sequences ground language models with measurable functional reality. We argue that progress in this multimodal direction will be driven by three coordinated strategies — (i) building strong unimodal genomic foundation models that scale long-context nucleotide sequence understanding, (ii) bridging text and sequence modalities through shared embeddings, instruction tuning, and text-conditioned generation, and (iii) deploying the resulting multimodal systems on downstream applications such as variant-effect prediction, regulatory annotation, and sequence-aware drug discovery.

paradigms.label: Genomic Foundation Models (DNA / RNA)
paradigms.summary: Unimodal foundation models for nucleotide sequences are the building blocks of any text-genomic multimodal system. Tokenization choices, long-context architectures, and self-supervised pretraining objectives determine what these models can learn and how well they transfer to downstream tasks.

claims.claim: Tokenization strategy materially affects what genomic foundation models can learn — k-mer, BPE, and single-nucleotide schemes induce distinct biases on context length, motif resolution, and downstream transfer.
claims.search_terms: DNA tokenization k-mer BPE nucleotide foundation model

claims.claim: Long-context architectures (state-space models, Hyena, Mamba, sliding-window attention) unlock genome-scale sequence modeling well beyond traditional Transformer context limits, enabling distal regulatory interactions to be learned end-to-end.
claims.search_terms: long context genomic state space model Hyena Mamba foundation

claims.claim: Self-supervised pretraining objectives — masked language modeling, autoregressive generation, span corruption, contrastive learning — determine downstream transfer quality and the kinds of biological structure (motifs, conservation, splicing, 3D contacts) the model captures.
claims.search_terms: self-supervised pretraining DNA RNA masked language model autoregressive

paradigms.label: Text–Sequence Multimodal Integration
paradigms.summary: Bridging natural-language models and genomic foundation models so each can ground the other. Text supplies literature context and high-level intent; sequences supply measurable functional reality. Three integration patterns: shared embedding spaces, instruction tuning, and text-conditioned sequence generation.

claims.claim: Shared embedding spaces between natural language and biological sequences enable cross-modal retrieval, sequence-grounded question answering, and unified inference across literature and experimental data.
claims.search_terms: joint embedding text DNA RNA biological sequence multimodal contrastive

claims.claim: Instruction tuning lets language models follow user queries that reference DNA / RNA sequences, condition on sequence inputs, and produce structured biological reasoning grounded in the input sequence.
claims.search_terms: instruction tuning genomic biological large language model sequence input

claims.claim: Text-conditioned sequence generation — describing a desired biological function, regulatory context, or therapeutic target in natural language and having the model propose candidate DNA / RNA sequences — turns LLM-style natural-language interfaces into a primitive of biological sequence design.
claims.search_terms: text guided sequence generation biological design DNA RNA conditional

paradigms.label: Downstream Multimodal Applications
paradigms.summary: What multimodal text-genomic models actually deliver. Three high-leverage downstream targets: variant-effect prediction, regulatory-element annotation with natural-language explanations, and sequence-aware drug / therapeutic discovery.

claims.claim: Multimodal text-genomic models predict variant pathogenicity and functional effects with the help of text-supervised priors (clinical literature, phenotype descriptions, pathway ontologies) that pure sequence models cannot access.
claims.search_terms: variant effect prediction language model genomic clinical pathogenicity

claims.claim: Joint text-and-sequence models annotate regulatory elements — enhancers, promoters, splice sites, binding sites — with natural-language explanations that go beyond categorical labels.
claims.search_terms: regulatory element annotation enhancer promoter splice multimodal explanation

claims.claim: Multimodal text-genomic models accelerate drug target identification, RNA-therapeutic design, and clinical hypothesis generation by linking the literature corpus to candidate biological sequences in a single generative system.
claims.search_terms: drug discovery RNA therapeutic sequence design language model literature

venue_allowlist: Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences

viewer.title: Multimodal NLP × Genomic Foundation Models — Knowledge Graph
viewer.subtitle: Three coordinated strategies for combining language models with DNA / RNA foundation models — discovered, scored, ranked, and evidence-linked.
